initial_test_output:
=== TLS Chain Ordering Test ===
--- Building ---
--- Starting HTTPS server ---
Starting HTTPS server on :8443
--- Test 1: Lenient TLS client ---
OK: Lenient client connected successfully
--- Test 2: Chain order verification (strict RFC 5246) ---
FAIL: Chain order violation at position 0:
      cert[0].Issuer  = issuer=C=US, ST=Test, L=Test, O=TestIntermediateCA, CN=Test Intermediate CA
      cert[1].Subject = subject=C=US, ST=Test, L=Test, O=TestRootCA, CN=Test Root CA
      Expected these to match (cert[1] should be the issuer of cert[0])
FAIL: Chain order violation at position 1:
      cert[1].Issuer  = issuer=C=US, ST=Test, L=Test, O=TestRootCA, CN=Test Root CA
      cert[2].Subject = subject=C=US, ST=Test, L=Test, O=TestIntermediateCA, CN=Test Intermediate CA
      Expected these to match (cert[2] should be the issuer of cert[1])
--- Test 3: Leaf certificate identity ---
OK: First certificate is the leaf (CN=localhost)

FAIL: TLS chain ordering issues detected

output_preview: bash: line 1: test.sh: command not found
